title: Analysis scope of your project | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# Setting the analysis scope of your project

## [Step 1: Check your project's default initial scope](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/introduction/#check-initial-scope "Step 1: Check your project's default initial scope")

Check your project's default initial scope. If it doesn't suit you, you must set the initial scope explicitly.

See [Setting the initial analysis scope of a project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/setting-initial-scope/ "Setting the initial analysis scope of a project").

## [Step 2: If necessary, adjust the initial scope](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/introduction/#adjust-initial-scope "Step 2: If necessary, adjust the initial scope")

You may have to adjust the initial scope mainly in the following cases:

- If the directory structure of your project does not cleanly separate source code from test code at the top level. In that case, you can exclude files based on path-matching patterns or file extensions by programming language.
- To exclude specific files from duplication check.
- To exclude specific files from coverage reporting.

You can also perform other exclusions, such as excluding files over a certain size limit or disabling the SCM’s file ignore patterns. In very few situations, you may have to use advanced exclusion features.

To exclude code from the analysis, you define exclusion parameters for the project. You can define most exclusion parameters:

- In SonarQube Cloud UI
  This requires that you have the Administer permission for the project.
  When possible, it is the recommended method since it allows a centralized, reliable, and user-friendly configuration.
- On the CI/CD host
  With this method, you configure the respective sonar properties on the CI/CD host. Note that a parameter set through this method has precedence over any UI setting of the same parameter.

See:

- [Excluding specific files from your project's code coverage analysis or duplication check](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/exclude-from-coverage-duplication/ "Excluding specific files from your project's code coverage analysis or duplication check")
- [Excluding files from your project analysis based on path-matching patterns](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/excluding-files-based-on-patterns/ "Excluding files from your project analysis based on path-matching patterns")
- [Excluding files from your project analysis based on file extension](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/excluding-based-on-file-extension/ "Excluding files from your project analysis based on file extension")
- [Applying advanced exclusions to your project analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/advanced-exclusions/ "Applying advanced exclusions to your project analysis")
- [Performing other analysis scope adjustments](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/other-adjustments/ "Performing other analysis scope adjustments")

## [Step3: Verify the settings](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/introduction/#verify-settings "Step3: Verify the settings")

After the first project analysis run, you can verify that the configured analysis scope is correct. See [Verifying the analysis scope of a project.](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-your-projects/project-analysis/setting-analysis-scope/verifying-analysis-scope/ "Verifying the analysis scope of a project.")
